## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## jina
Web access and online content retrieval server.

WHEN TO USE THIS SERVER:

Web Search (use when user wants to find something ONLINE, not local files):
- "search the web for...", "google...", "look up online...", "find on the internet..."
- "what's the latest news on...", "current events about...", "recent updates on..."
- Any query needing real-time or up-to-date information from the internet

URL/Webpage Reading (use when user provides a URL or link):
- "read this URL: https://...", "what does this webpage say...", "summarize this link..."
- "fetch the content from...", "extract text from this website..."
- Any task involving a specific URL, http link, or webpage

Academic Paper Search (use for scholarly/research queries):
- "search arXiv for...", "find papers on arXiv about..."
- "search SSRN for...", "find economics/finance/law papers..."
- "find academic papers about...", "what research exists on..."

BibTeX Citations (use when user needs citation/bibliography entries):
- "get bibtex for...", "find citation for this paper..."
- "search for bibtex entries...", "get bibliography entry for..."
- Any request for LaTeX citations or academic references in BibTeX format

Image Search (use when user wants to find images online):
- "search for images of...", "find pictures of...", "find photos of..."

Screenshot Capture (use when user wants to SEE a webpage):
- "take a screenshot of this URL...", "capture this webpage visually..."
- "show me what this website looks like..."

Text Classification:
- "classify this text as...", "categorize these texts into...", "is this text positive or negative..."
- "label these documents...", "sentiment analysis of..."

Semantic Reranking/Deduplication:
- "rerank these results by relevance to...", "sort by semantic similarity..."
- "deduplicate these texts/images...", "find unique items from..."

PDF Extraction:
- "extract figures from this PDF...", "get tables from PDF...", "extract equations from PDF..."

NOT FOR: local file operations, code execution, database queries, non-web APIs… [truncated]

## perplexity
Perplexity AI server for web-grounded search, research, and reasoning. Use perplexity_search for finding URLs, facts, and recent news. Use perplexity_ask for quick AI-answered questions with citations. Supports recency filters, domain restrictions, and search context size control. Use perplexity_research for in-depth multi-source investigation (slow, 30s+). Supports reasoning_effort parameter to control depth. Use perplexity_reason for complex analysis requiring step-by-step logic. Supports recency filters, domain restrictions, and search context size control. All tools are read-only and access live web data.

- research: PROACTIVE: invoke on ANY search/research request. Triggers: "поищи", "найди", "узнай", "исследуй", "ресёрч", "изучи", "собери", "что известно", "расскажи про", "сравни", "search", "find out", "research", "investigate", "compare", "look into", "what is", "академический ресёрч", "papers", "scientific", "literature review". Two modes: general (web + YouTube + NotebookLM) and academic (PubMed + Semantic Scholar + OpenAlex with journal quality + predatory filter). Confirm mode before executing.
- roadmap-decompose: Decompose a large brief or PRD into phases and parallelizable backlog tasks written to `docs/ROADMAP.md`. The roadmap is then materialized into phase files + backlog task files via `aif roadmap import` (Python CLI in ai-factory). Skill side handles only LLM decomposition; Python side handles parsing/file I/O. TRIGGER on: "/roadmap-decompose", "сделай roadmap", "разбей на фазы", "decompose roadmap", "break this into phases", "create roadmap", after a PRD or large brief is written. ANTI-TRIGGER: small fixes (single feature, single file), trivial tasks → use `aif phase new` directly. Vague ideas without a PRD/brief → use `/discovery` first.
- avoid-ai-writing: Audit and rewrite content to remove AI writing patterns ("AI-isms"). Use this skill when asked to "remove AI-isms," "clean up AI writing," "edit writing for AI patterns," "audit writing for AI tells," or "make this sound less like AI." Supports a detection-only mode that flags patterns without rewriting.
- skill-evolve: Collect lessons from all ~/coding/*/.claude/projects/*/memory/, route them to relevant skills, publish a weekly proposal with diffs to mike-skill-pack/skills/<name>/SKILL.md. Apply happens ONLY after explicit user approve via /skill-evolve apply <date>. TRIGGER on: "/skill-evolve", "/skill-evolve apply YYYY-MM-DD", "evolve skills", "прокачай скиллы". Auto-runs via cron `0 10 * * 1` (Mondays 10am) — generates proposal + Telegram notification. ANTI-TRIGGER: trivial fixes, single-session experiments, lessons about external services (not model).
- test-and-fix: Dev-time QA loop: open a real browser, exercise critical user flows, find bugs, fix them in code, atomic-commit each fix, verify regression. Iterates until clean or self-stop limits hit. For your own products in dev/staging, not for production audits of third-party sites.
TRIGGER on "test the site", "qa this", "проверь сайт", "найди баги", "test-and-fix", a working dev/staging URL handed in.
ANTI-TRIGGER: production third-party site audits → use `qa-audit` (Playwright config-based, report-only).
- freeze: Lock Edit/Write to one directory inside a repo. Behavioral by default — Claude self-enforces from `.claude/freeze-boundary.txt`. Strict mode (PreToolUse hook) is opt-in via explicit user verification.
TRIGGER on "freeze edits to X", "lock scope to X", "только в X", "не трогай ничего кроме X", "заморозь область", "/freeze".
ANTI-TRIGGER: vague requests like "be careful" — that's a different concern, not this skill.
- shadcn-ui: Expert guidance for integrating and building applications with shadcn/ui components, including component discovery, installation, customization, and best practices.
- research-add-fields: Add field definitions to existing research outline.
- code-discipline: Behavioral discipline for any code work — writing, editing, refactoring, reviewing, debugging. Forces explicit assumptions, blocks overengineering, keeps changes surgical. TRIGGER on Russian commands ("напиши функцию", "реализуй", "добавь модуль", "исправь баг", "поправь", "отрефактори", "перепиши", "допиши", "ревью кода", "напиши тест", "сделай скрипт") and English equivalents ("write code", "implement", "add function/class/module", "fix bug", "refactor", "edit code", "review PR", "build feature", "write tests"). Applies to source code, scripts, infrastructure-as-code (terraform, n8n nodes, k8s manifests, docker-compose), tests, migrations. SKIP for vault notes, Linear issues, prose docs, README, commit messages, MCP/CLI configuration edits, planning, chat answers. Based on Karpathy's LLM coding observations.
- lesson-learned: Зафиксировать урок из ошибки/сбоя в правильное хранилище (memory / vault insights / global rules / project CLAUDE.md) на основе типа ошибки. 5 типов: config, interpretation, skip-check, wrong-tool, edge-case (см. references/error-types.md). TRIGGER on: "/lesson", "запиши урок", "lesson learned", "compounding engineering", или когда юзер 3+ раз в одной задаче пишет "не так" / "переделай" / "стоп" / "это не то". Также активируется через Stop hook при провале тестов/линта/pre-commit. ANTI-TRIGGER: одиночные опечатки, тривиальные правки, ошибки из-за внешнего сервиса (не модели).
- research-report: Summarize deep research results into markdown report, cover all fields, skip uncertain values.
- llm-proxy-check: PROACTIVE: invoke before writing or modifying any code that calls an LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic/Claude, Gemini/Google, Mistral, Cohere, Groq), before curl smoke-tests of LLM keys, and when diagnosing LLM auth/geo errors. Triggers (any): "OpenAI", "Anthropic", "Gemini", "Claude API", "openai sdk", "anthropic sdk", "generativeai", "httpx LLM", "LLM client", "API key rotat", "новый ключ OpenAI", "ротировать ключ", "test the LLM key", "проверить ключ OpenAI", errors "unsupported_country_region_territory", "Country not supported", "PERMISSION_DENIED" (Gemini), "CONSUMER_SUSPENDED". Skip for: general research, prompt-engineering-only discussions, code that does NOT call an LLM.
- bootstrap-repo: Bootstrap a new repository with unified, agent-readable documentation. Generates `README.md` (root), `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` (skeleton), and a vault note `cb-<name>.md` in `~/coding/SecondBrain/_inbox/`. Updates `projects/codebase-info/index.md`. Files only — does not run git/commit/push.
TRIGGER on "bootstrap repo", "init project docs", "инициализируй проект", "сделай документацию для нового репо", after `gh repo create` or fresh `git init`.
ANTI-TRIGGER: existing repos with non-empty README/docs — propose `/doc-sync` instead.
- code-review-loop: Dual-model code quality loop: Claude Code implements, Codex reviews. Iterates until code meets world-class engineering standards. PROACTIVE: Auto-trigger after significant code changes (3+ files or 50+ lines of code modified). Auto-trigger before commits with substantial code diffs. Also trigger when the pre-commit hook reminder appears in context. TRIGGER on: "review loop", "code review loop", "write and review", "dual review", "implement and review", "codex review", "напиши и отревьюй", "цикл ревью", "двойная проверка", or when you just finished writing a significant feature/fix/refactor. Requires: codex-plugin-cc installed, Codex CLI authenticated.
- skill-from-docs: Generate a Claude Code skill draft from a documentation URL. Fetches via jina, delegates extraction to local-worker (Ollama qwen3:14b), assembles a canonical mike-skill-pack SKILL.md draft into `~/coding/mike-skill-pack/skills/drafts/<slug>/`. Draft is NOT auto-linked into ~/.claude/skills/ — user polishes and links manually. TRIGGER on: "/skill-from-docs <url>", "сделай скилл из <url>", "make a skill from <url>", "create skill from documentation", "skill from URL". ANTI-TRIGGER: vague topic without URL → propose `/discovery` first; libraries with existing official docs MCP (use that instead).
- audit-claude-rules: Audit ~/.claude/rules/* и ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md под Bitter Lesson — найти инструкции, которые Opus 4.7+ выполняет нативно. Пишет markdown-отчёт в ~/coding/SecondBrain/_inbox/, никогда не правит rules сам. Также детектит дубли и устаревшие правила в lessons-learned.md. TRIGGER on: "/audit-claude-rules", "audit rules", "проверь правила", "audit claude-md". Запускается еженедельно через schedule routine (cron 0 9 * * 1). ANTI-TRIGGER: единичные правки rules-файлов вручную.
- discovery-interview: Структурный сбор требований для новой задачи / проекта / идеи через 4 универсальных meta-вопроса (цель / пользователи / observable success / стек) + on-the-fly уточнения по ответам. На выходе TASK.md или PRD.md с обязательной секцией Verification criteria. TRIGGER on: "/discovery", "discovery interview", "discovery", "опиши идею", "сформулируй задачу", автозапуск из bootstrap-repo при создании нового репо. ANTI-TRIGGER: тривиальные правки, баги в одном файле, опечатки.
- research-add-items: Add items (research objects) to existing research outline.
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- mental-models: PROACTIVE on major decisions, suggest 1-line. новый проект/цель/фича→pre-mortem; архитектура/стэк/MCP→inversion; релиз/цена→red-team; стоит ли/колеблюсь→10-10-10. ANTI: bug/typo/refactor/routine/layer-3/freeze.
- team-architect: Designs and executes multi-agent team architectures for Claude Code. TRIGGER on: "create a team", "design agents", "multi-agent", "team architecture", "set up teammates", "спроектировать команду", "создай команду", "параллельно". Also suggest when: large implementation tasks with 3+ independent workstreams, full-stack features, cross-module refactors, or migrations.
- doc-sync: Sync documentation after a code diff on a feature branch. Updates `README.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `CLAUDE.md` inside the repo, and the matching `cb-<repo>.md` note in `~/coding/SecondBrain/projects/codebase-info/` if it exists.
TRIGGER on "sync docs", "update docs after ship", "обнови доки", "сверь документацию", typically after `/commit` on a feature branch and before `/push`.
ANTI-TRIGGER: empty repo or no docs to sync — propose `/bootstrap-repo` instead.
- deploy-loop: Autonomous deployment cycle for Directual platform. Agent deploys code into Web Builder via Chrome DevTools, then verifies on live site. Human-in-the-loop for backend config only.
- code-optimizer: Optimize code for safety, readability, and performance within the stack.
- doc-optimizer: Optimize documentation for minimal context length and token usage while preserving meaning, with step-by-step approvals.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- directual-backend: Database structures, API endpoints, scenarios, business logic for Directual NoSQL platform. Use when working with data models, DQL queries, or backend scenarios.
- directual-core: Platform fundamentals, constraints, limitations, template system syntax. Use when you need to understand Directual platform constraints or template mechanics before implementation.
- ru-updater: Update Russian documentation copies under docs-ru/ after English docs change. Use when user edits README.md, CLAUDE.md, or any .claude/**/*.md file. Use when user says "update Russian docs", "sync translations", "перевести документацию".
- directual-frontend: UI components, Web-Page Builder, HTML/CSS/JS customization, Mustache templates. Use when building or modifying user interfaces, forms, tables, or custom frontend logic.
- push: Git push — auto-push with smart branch protection
- commit: Git commit — Conventional Commits, bilingual, no confirmation
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- firecrawl:skill-gen: Generate a complete Agent Skill from a documentation URL using Firecrawl
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- cc-gemini-plugin:gemini: Invoke the shared Gemini bridge for long-context code exploration, analysis, and documentation generation
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- firecrawl:firecrawl-map: Discover and list all URLs on a website, with optional search filtering. Use this skill when the user wants to find a specific page on a large site, list all URLs, see the site structure, find where something is on a domain, or says "map the site", "find the URL for", "what pages are on", or "list all pages". Essential when the user knows which site but not which exact page.
- firecrawl:firecrawl-agent: AI-powered autonomous data extraction that navigates complex sites and returns structured JSON. Use this skill when the user wants structured data from websites, needs to extract pricing tiers, product listings, directory entries, or any data as JSON with a schema. Triggers on "extract structured data", "get all the products", "pull pricing info", "extract as JSON", or when the user provides a JSON schema for website data. More powerful than simple scraping for multi-page structured extraction.
- firecrawl:firecrawl-scrape: Extract clean markdown from any URL, including JavaScript-rendered SPAs. Use this skill whenever the user provides a URL and wants its content, says "scrape", "grab", "fetch", "pull", "get the page", "extract from this URL", or "read this webpage". Handles JS-rendered pages, multiple concurrent URLs, and returns LLM-optimized markdown. Use this instead of WebFetch for any webpage content extraction.
- firecrawl:firecrawl-download: Download an entire website as local files — markdown, screenshots, or multiple formats per page. Use this skill when the user wants to save a site locally, download documentation for offline use, bulk-save pages as files, or says "download the site", "save as local files", "offline copy", "download all the docs", or "save for reference". Combines site mapping and scraping into organized local directories.
- firecrawl:firecrawl-instruct: Control and interact with a live browser session on any scraped page — click buttons, fill forms, navigate flows, and extract data using natural language prompts or code. Replaces the old firecrawl-browser command. Use when the user needs to interact with a webpage beyond simple scraping: logging into a site, submitting forms, clicking through pagination, handling infinite scroll, navigating multi-step checkout or wizard flows, or when a regular scrape failed because content is behind JavaScript interaction. Also useful for authenticated scraping via profiles. Triggers on "browser", "instruct", "click", "fill out the form", "log in to", "sign in", "submit", "paginated", "next page", "infinite scroll", "interact with the page", "navigate to", "open a session", or "scrape failed".
- firecrawl:firecrawl-crawl: Bulk extract content from an entire website or site section. Use this skill when the user wants to crawl a site, extract all pages from a docs section, bulk-scrape multiple pages following links, or says "crawl", "get all the pages", "extract everything under /docs", "bulk extract", or needs content from many pages on the same site. Handles depth limits, path filtering, and concurrent extraction.
- firecrawl:firecrawl-cli: Web scraping, search, crawling, and page interaction via the Firecrawl CLI. Use this skill whenever the user wants to search the web, find articles, research a topic, look something up online, scrape a webpage, grab content from a URL, extract data from a website, crawl documentation, download a site, or interact with pages that need clicks or logins. Also use when they say "fetch this page", "pull the content from", "get the page at https://", or reference scraping external websites. This provides real-time web search with full page content extraction and interact capabilities — beyond what Claude can do natively with built-in tools. Do NOT trigger for local file operations, git commands, deployments, or code editing tasks.
- firecrawl:firecrawl-search: Web search with full page content extraction. Use this skill whenever the user asks to search the web, find articles, research a topic, look something up, find recent news, discover sources, or says "search for", "find me", "look up", "what are people saying about", or "find articles about". Returns real search results with optional full-page markdown — not just snippets. Provides capabilities beyond Claude's built-in WebSearch.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- cc-gemini-plugin:gemini-integration: Use Gemini CLI for long-context codebase exploration, architecture review, refactor impact analysis, documentation synthesis, or structured data analysis when the host should hand off a large cross-file problem instead of solving it file-by-file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# userCw

User's client workspaces. Structure: `clientsWorkspaces`. Access: all signed-in users.

## GET Fields

- `id`, `clientWorkspaceName` (visible name), `clientWorkspaceOwner` → WebUser

## GET Filters (OR)

All use `like {{id}}` (current user):
`clientEvidpoOwners`, `clientEvidpoAdmins`, `clientEvidpoWorkers`, `usersByClient`, `clientWorkspaceOwner`

## POST — Read-only

0 fields selected. Available but disabled: `id`, `cw_reestr_created`, `clientCompanies`, `clientWorkspaceName`, `clientOrders` + 7 more.

## Usage

```js
window.callEndpoint('userCw', 'GET', undefined, { pageSize: 50 }, cb);
// Find workspace by cwId → extract clientWorkspaceOwner.id
```

# API Endpoints Reference

Source of truth for all Directual endpoints used in the portal.

## Status Legend
- `LIVE` — created on Directual, tested, working
- `TODO` — not created yet or config is incomplete (for example no writable fields)

## Endpoint Registry

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Status | Doc |
|----------|--------|---------|--------|-----|
| `cwreestr_students_list` | GET | Fetch students list | LIVE | students.md |
| `cwreestr_students_list` | POST | Update personal data | LIVE | students.md |
| `scopesList` | GET | Fetch training scopes | LIVE | — |
| `cwreestr_docs_list` | GET | Fetch docs by student | LIVE | cwreestr_docs_list.md |
| `dd_order2_contragents` | GET | Fetch companies (active only — auto-hides `is_delete=true`) | LIVE | dd_order2_contragents.md |
| `dd_order2_contragents` | POST | Create / update / soft-delete company | LIVE | dd_order2_contragents.md |
| `contragents_all` | GET | Fetch companies INCLUDING archived (Архив segment) | LIVE | contragents_all.md |
| `cwreestr_orders` | GET | Fetch orders list/detail (Orders2 UI) | LIVE | orders2.md |
| `orders2` | POST | Create/update request basic fields | LIVE | orders2.md |
| `student_courses` | GET | Fetch student-course links | LIVE | student_courses.md |
| `student_courses` | POST | Create/update student-course links | TODO | student_courses.md |
| `dd_students_job_titles` | GET | Search job titles | LIVE | — |
| `dd_students_job_titles` | POST | Create/update job title | LIVE | — |
| `cwreestr_employments` | POST | Create employment | LIVE | employments.md |
| `cwreestr_employments` | POST | Update employment (dismiss/restore) | LIVE | employments.md |
| `dd_courses2` | GET | Fetch courses by scope | LIVE | — |
| `cwreestr_doctypes_list` | GET | Fetch non-course doc types by scope | LIVE | cwreestr_doctypes_list.md |
| `userCw` | GET | User's workspaces (clientWorkspaceOwner) | LIVE | userCw.md |

## Rules
- Always verify endpoint status before writing `callEndpoint()` code
- If status is `TODO` — create/fix endpoint config on Directual first, then update status here
- Field names in code must match endpoint field mapping on Directual

## Common Patterns

### Authentication
All API calls use `window.callEndpoint()` with session handling.
**Exception — file uploads (FormData)**: use direct fetch to Directual REST API.
callEndpoint JSON.stringify's the body → FormData becomes {} → "No file provided".
Pattern: `fetch('https://api.directual.com/good/api/v5/data/<structure>/<endpoint>?appID=X&sessionID=Y', { method:'POST', body: formData })`

### Workspace Isolation
All endpoints require workspace filter (`cw_id` / `cwid`).

### Error Handling
```json
{ "isError": true, "msg": "Error description" }
```

Я разобрался с контекстом. Текущая страница `/cw` — Directual-страница, которая через `userCw` (GET) рисует карточки рабочих областей. Локального исходника у неё в `src/` нет. Эндпоинт `userCw` существует (LIVE) и возвращает `id`, `clientWorkspaceName`, `clientWorkspaceOwner`.

**Краткий план:**

<script>
(function () {
  var html = document.documentElement;
  html.style.visibility = 'hidden';
  var revealed = false;
  function reveal() { if (revealed) return; revealed = true; html.style.visibility = ''; }
  var safety = setTimeout(reveal, 3000);

  var tries = 0;
  function run() {
    if (!window.callEndpoint) {
      if (++tries > 60) { clearTimeout(safety); reveal(); return; }
      setTimeout(run, 50);
      return;
    }
    try {
      window.callEndpoint('userCw', 'GET', undefined, { pageSize: 50 }, function (result, resData) {
        try {
          var raw = resData && (resData.payload || resData.result || resData);
          var data = Array.isArray(raw) ? raw : (raw && Array.isArray(raw.data) ? raw.data : []);
          if (data.length === 1 && data[0] && data[0].id) {
            clearTimeout(safety);
            window.location.replace('/cw/' + encodeURIComponent(data[0].id));
            return;
          }
        } catch (e) {}
        clearTimeout(safety);
        reveal();
      });
    } catch (e) {
      clearTimeout(safety);
      reveal();
    }
  }
  run();
})();
</script>

# 2026-05-15 — Employees: Company filter popover

## RU

На странице «Сотрудники» в шапке колонки «КОМПАНИЯ» иконка‑фильтр теперь рабочая. По клику открывается поповер с мульти‑выбором компаний:

- Поиск по названию компании внутри поповера.
- Чек‑бокс на каждую компанию + счётчик сотрудников.
- Кнопка «Сбросить» очищает выбор.
- На кнопке колонки появляется синий бэйдж с количеством выбранных компаний.
- Закрытие: клик вне поповера или клавиша Esc.

### Логика

- Список компаний строится из активных трудоустройств всех видимых карточек (`script.employment-json` → `contragent_name`), отфильтрованных по текущему `cw_id`.
- Карточка проходит фильтр, если хотя бы одно из её трудоустройств принадлежит выбранной компании (OR между выбранными значениями).
- Фильтр применяется на клиенте поверх существующих: «Работающие / Уволенные», статус документов, поиск по ФИО / должности (DQL на сервере). Все условия комбинируются по AND.
- Выбор хранится на `window._cwFilter.companyFilter` — переживает перерисовки Directual.

## EN

Activated the existing column‑filter icon for «КОМПАНИЯ» on the employees registry. Multi‑select popover with in‑popover search, per‑company counts, reset button, blue badge on the column button.

Stacks with: working / fired tab, doc‑status dropdown and the ФИО / должность search input. Pure client‑side filtering over rendered cards; state persists on `window._cwFilter.companyFilter` across Directual re‑renders.

## Files

- `src/cw_cwid.html` — popover HTML inside «КОМПАНИЯ» header cell, new CSS block `.cf-pop*`, new JS helpers (`getAllCompanyNames`, `isCardMatchingCompanyFilter`, `renderCompanyPopover`, `openCompanyPopover` / `closeCompanyPopover` / `toggleCompanyPopover`, `updateCompanyButtonState`), bindings in `bindEvents()`, integration into `applyClientFilters()`.

## Verify

- Open «Сотрудники», click the filter icon next to «КОМПАНИЯ» → popover lists every company seen in the current workspace.
- Pick one company → other rows hide; badge shows `1`.
- Pick a second company → both companies' employees are visible (OR).
- Uncheck or press «Сбросить» → all eligible rows return.
- Combine with «Уволенные» tab + «Просрочено» status + ФИО search → results respect every active filter.
- Switch tabs / paginate → selection persists, badge stays, list rebuilds.

## Refs

# 2026-05-19 — /cw: auto-redirect when user has one workspace

## RU

На странице `/cw` (выбор рабочей области) добавлен авто-редирект: если у пользователя ровно одна рабочая область, его сразу перебрасывает на `/cw/<id>`. Если рабочих областей несколько — список показывается как раньше.

### Логика

- Сразу при загрузке скрываю `<html>` через `visibility:hidden` — пользователь не видит мерцания списка.
- Жду `window.callEndpoint` (poll до 3s).
- Вызываю `userCw` GET `pageSize=50`, парсю стандартно (`payload`/`result`/array).
- Ровно 1 workspace с `id` → `window.location.replace('/cw/' + id)`.
- Иначе → снимаю `visibility:hidden`, страница рендерится обычным образом.
- Safety-таймер 3s — если API не ответил, страница всё равно показывается.

## EN

On `/cw` (workspace picker), users with exactly one workspace are now auto-redirected to `/cw/<id>`. Users with 2+ workspaces still see the list.

- `visibility:hidden` on `<html>` until API responds → no flash.
- `userCw` GET → count results.
- 1 result with `id` → `window.location.replace('/cw/' + id)`.
- 0 or 2+ results → reveal page.
- 3s safety timeout to avoid blank screen on API hang.

## Files

- `src/cw_autoredirect.html` — new file, ~30 lines of inline JS. Injected as Custom HTML on the `/cw` page in Directual Web-Builder.

## Verify

- V1: Test user with 1 workspace opens `/cw` → instant redirect to `/cw/<id>`, no list flash.
- V2: Test user with 2+ workspaces opens `/cw` → list renders as before.
- V3: Throttle network or block `userCw` → after 3s page reveals with list.
- V4: `/cw/<id>` itself unaffected (snippet lives only on `/cw`).

## Refs

- Endpoint: `.claude/reference/api-endpoints/userCw.md`
- Redirect pattern reused from: `src/cw_orders2_detail.html:1287`
- Task: `docs/tasks/current/T-20260519-cw-single-workspace-autoredirect.md`

- `src/cw_autoredirect.html` — снипет для вставки как Custom HTML на странице `/cw` в Directual.

- `2e5d273` main: `feat(cw): auto-redirect to single workspace on /cw`
- `b4e7039` updates: `feed: auto-redirect to single workspace on /cw`

**Что осталось — деплой в Directual.** Снипет надо вставить как Custom HTML-блок на страницу `/cw` в Web-Builder. Для запуска: `/deploy-loop` (нужно включить Chrome DevTools MCP: `~/.claude/scripts/mcp-research.sh on` + рестарт сессии). После деплоя — проверить V1/V2/V3 из задачи и пометить статус `done`.

next_action: Implement src/cw_autoredirect.html, then deploy to /cw page via /deploy-loop.
---

## Goal

User lands on `/cw`. If they have only one workspace — instant redirect to `/cw/<id>`. Two clicks become zero. List remains for users with 2+ workspaces.

## Approach

Custom HTML/JS snippet injected on `/cw` page in Directual.

1. Immediately set `document.documentElement.style.visibility = 'hidden'` to hide flash.
2. Poll for `window.callEndpoint` availability (Directual session).
3. Call `userCw` GET with `pageSize=50`.
4. Parse via `Array.isArray(data) ? data : (data.payload || data.result || [])`.
5. If `data.length === 1` and has `id` → `window.location.replace('/cw/' + id)`.
6. Else → reveal page (`visibility:''`).
7. Safety timeout 3s — reveal anyway if API hangs.

No `DOMContentLoaded` — immediate exec per project rules.

## Files

- `src/cw_autoredirect.html` — standalone HTML+JS snippet, ≤1000 chars.

## Verifies

- V1: Test user with 1 workspace opens `/cw` → instant redirect to `/cw/<id>`, no list flash.
- V2: Test user with 2+ workspaces opens `/cw` → list renders as before.
- V3: Network throttled / API error — after 3s page reveals with list (no infinite blank).
- V4: No regression on `/cw/<id>` itself (snippet only on `/cw`).

## Refs

- Endpoint: `.claude/reference/api-endpoints/userCw.md`
- Pattern: `src/cw_orders2_detail.html:1287` (`window.location.replace`)
